data.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement core ci-llm-review-runner.sh with classifier-driven tier dispatch (light/standard) replacing the existing single direct_prompt call in .github/workflows/ci.yml.

**Why**: Walking skeleton — proves the new architecture in CI for the simplest tier. All other Epic C stories build on this foundation.

**Scope**:
- IN: ci-llm-review-runner.sh script; classifier invocation; light + standard tier code paths; named agent file load; findings via write-reviewer-findings.sh; record-review.sh integrity check; non-zero exit on score<4 or critical; DSO_LLM_TIER env var removed from workflow.
- OUT: deep tier (Story 2); overlays (Story 3); 3-tier version resolution + fetch (Story 4); auto-detect runner mode (Story 5); test PR verification (Story 6); host-project E2E (Story 7); docs cleanup (Story 8).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the CI llm-review job invokes review-complexity-classifier.sh against the PR diff and selects tier per PR (light or standard).
  ← Satisfies: "CI llm-review invokes review-complexity-classifier.sh against the PR diff to select tier per PR"
- When this story is complete, the named agent file (code-reviewer-light.md or code-reviewer-standard.md) is loaded verbatim as system prompt for the claude-code-action invocation.
  ← Satisfies: "Named agent files loaded verbatim as system prompt for each claude-code-action invocation"
- When this story is complete, the runner writes findings via write-reviewer-findings.sh, runs record-review.sh with --reviewer-hash, and exits non-zero on any score < 4 or critical finding.
  ← Satisfies: "Findings written via write-reviewer-findings.sh, verified by record-review.sh with --reviewer-hash; non-zero exit on score < 4 or critical finding"
- When this story is complete, DSO_LLM_TIER env var no longer appears in .github/workflows/ci.yml.
  ← Satisfies: "DSO_LLM_TIER env var removed"
- When this story is complete, the runner exposes a documented overlay-flag passthrough contract that consumes security_overlay/performance_overlay/test_quality_overlay flags from the classifier output and emits them in a form Story 3 can consume.
  ← Satisfies: dependency contract for overlay dispatch (Story 3 prerequisite)
- unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] New helper script ci-llm-review-runner.sh must follow plugin patterns (_PLUGIN_ROOT pattern, no hardcoded paths).
- [Reliability] Classifier failure handling must follow existing classifier-failure-invariant — non-zero exit or invalid JSON → default to standard tier.
- [Conflicting-assumptions] Classifier diff source in CI must explicitly construct the PR base...HEAD merge-base diff (not HEAD~1 or working tree). The CI checkout action may fetch a shallow clone or check out a merge commit; document the exact git invocation that produces the diff input. Diff source contract must match what the agents see at review time.
- [Implicit-shared-state] record-review.sh hash verification depends on byte-identical diffs across the runner, the agents, and record-review.sh. Pin exact diff invocation flags (--no-color, whitespace, rename detection, encoding); ensure all three consume the same diff bytes. Hash mismatches in this scenario have been a recurring incident class locally (see feedback_review_gate_hash memory).
- [Consumer-impact] DSO_LLM_TIER removal is a breaking change for any host project that has set it. Add a deprecation warning when the var is detected (one release cycle), then remove. Match the soft-deprecation pattern used by DSO_TICKET_LEGACY.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a project maintainer, I can rely on the CI llm-review job using the same classifier-driven tier selection as local /dso:review for light and standard tiers
